notes_from_pm: formerly XII
doorways in outer part usurped from Ramesses V.
Inaccessible after Corridor B.
Temp. Merneptaḥ-Siptaḥ.
wife of Sethos II. Usurped by Setnakht.
formerly XI
son of Ramesses IX.
Excavated by Davis, and by Carnarvon and Carter.
Excavated by Belzoni.
1st ed. 24
Standard-bearer, Child of the nursery. Temp. Ḥatshepsut. Excavated by Loret.
Excavated by Loret.

Uninscribed tomb, attributed to Amenophis I by Weigall in Ann. Serv. xi (1911), pp. 174-5 [12], and id. A Guide to the Antiquities of Upper Egypt, pp. 163-4, but this is not supported by any inscriptional evidence, and does not correspond with the position given in the Abbott Papyrus (cf. Peet, The Great Tomb-Robberies of the Twentieth Egyptian Dynasty, pp. 37-8). See also the tomb of Queen ʿAhmosi Nefertere, infra, p. 599.
(?). Excavated by Loret
Excavated by Davis.
Overseer of the Fields of Amūn, Dyn. XVIII, re-used by Merenkhons, Doorkeeper of the House of Amūn, Dyn. XXII (name from scarab). Excavated by Davis and Carter.
Divine father, and Chief of the harîm of Amūn, parents of Queen Teye.
Governor of the town, Vizier. Temp. Amenophis II. (Also owner of Theb. tb. 29.)
Probably Amenophis IV, formerly attributed to Queen Teye or to Smenkhkarēʿ.
'Gold Tomb', uninscribed.
1st ed. 58. Excavated by Carnarvon and Carter.
Dyn. XX(?). Buried. (L. D. Text, No. 14, Bibl. i, 1st ed. No. 37.)
A PRINCESS, no name.
wife of Ramesses I. Unfinished. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 7, L. D. Text, No. 13, WILKINSON, No. 19.)
A QUEEN, cartouche blank. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 6, L. D. Text, No. 12, WILKINSON, No. 18.)
Charioteer of the stable of the Great House, son of Ramesses III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 8, L. D. Text, No. 11, WILKINSON, No. 16, HAY, No. 1.)
King's son, Hereditary prince of the royal children of his Majesty, Charioteer of the Great Stable, &c., son of Ramesses III.
Sem-priest of Ptaḥ, son of Ramesses III.
(probably). Vizier. Temp. Tuthmosis I. (Bibl. i, 1st ed. p. 49.)
daughter of Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa and Sit-ḏḥout. Dyn. XVII. (Bibl. i, 1st ed. p. 49.)
mother of Ramesses VI, daughter of Ḥubalzanet (i.e. Ḥemzert). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 4, L. D. Text, No. 10, WILKINSON, No. 14.)
Ramesside. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 3, L. D. Text, No. 9, WILKINSON, No. 12, HAY, No. 2.)
son of Ramesses III. (L. D. Text, No. 8, WILKINSON, No. 11, HAY, No. 3.)
Royal scribe, Overseer of horses, son of Ramesses III.
(?). daughter of Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 11, L. D. Text, No. 6, HAY, 2nd tomb east.)
wife of Ramesses II.
daughter of Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 12, L. D. Text, No. 5, HAY, No. 4.)
daughter of Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 13, L. D. Text, No. 4, HAY, No. 5.)
A PRINCESS, no name. Dyn. XX. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 14, L. D. Text, No. 3, HAY, No. 6.)
Great King's mother and King's wife. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 15, L. D. Text, No. 2, HAY, No. 7.)
A QUEEN, no name. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 16, L. D. Text, No. 1, HAY, No. 8.)
Servant in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XIX. Father, Khaʿbekhnet (name on fragment, BRUYÈRE, Rapport (1927), fig. 34 [4]). Wife, Iyneferti.
Servant in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 107.) Parents, Sennezem (tomb 1) and Iyneferti. Wives, Saḥte and (probably) Esi.
Servant in the Place of Truth on the west of Thebes. Ramesside. Parents, Menna and Huy. Wife, Nezemtbehdet.
Chiseller of Amūn in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 106.) Parents, Thonūfer, Chiseller of Amūn in the Khenu, and Maʿetnefert. Wives, Nefertere and Ḥenutmehyt.
Servant in the Place of Truth on the west of Thebes. Ramesside. Parents, Neferronpet and Mahi (name on stela in Brit. Mus. 150, see infra, p. 14). Wife, Taēsi.
Foremen in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ḥaremḥab to Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 101.) Wife (of Neferḥōtep), Iymau; (of Nebnūfer), Iy.
Scribe in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 99.) Parents, Amenemḥab and Kakaia. Wife, Mutemwia.
Chief in the Great Place. Temp. Amenophis II, Tuthmosis IV, and Amenophis III. (L. D. Text, No. 96.) Wife, Meryt.
Servant in the Place of Truth, Charmer of scorpions. Ramesside. Wife, Tent-hōm.

Servants in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 97.) Father (of Penbuy), Iri (name from offering-table of Penbuy, in Turin Mus. 1559). Wives (of Penbuy), Amentetusert and Irnūfer; (of Kasa), Bukhaʿnef.
Overseer of the treasury, Overseer of works. Temp. Ḥatshepsut to Tuthmosis III. (L. D. Text, No. 1.) Mother, Dediu.
Overseer of the granary of the King's wife and King's mother ʿAḥḥotp. Temp. Amosis to Amenophis I (?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 51, L. D. Text, No. 2.) Mother, ʿAḥmosi.
Head of brazier-bearers of Amūn. Ramesside. Wife, Wernūfer.
wʿab-priest of 'Amenophis, the favourite of Amūn'. Ramesside.
King's son, Mayor in the Southern City. Early Dyn. XVIII. Parents, Raḥotp, Overseer of the harim of the Lake (i.e. Fayûm), and Sensonb. Wife, Senbi.
Prophet of 'Amenophis of the Forecourt'. Temp. Ramesses II. Wife, Ternūte.
Scribe and physician of the King. Temp. Amenophis II (?). Parents, Nebseny, Judge, and Amenḥotp (?). Wife, Ta...nūfer.
Chief servant who weighs the silver and gold of the estate of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III or earlier. Father, a Scribe of counting of cattle of Queen ʿAḥmosi Nefertere. Wife, Mosi.
First prophet of 'Amenophis of the Forecourt'. Temp. Ramesses I to Sethos I (?). Wife, Iuy, Chief of the harim of Amenophis.
Fan-bearer, Mayor of Aphroditopolis. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?). Mother, Taysent.
Scribe, Steward of Tuthmosis I. Temp. Tuthmosis I. Wife, Bakt.
Royal butler. Partly usurped by Mery[amūn], Eldest son of the King. Temp. Tuthmosis III(?). Wife (of Mery[amūn]), Ḥatshepsut.

Royal scribe of the dispatches of the Lord of the Two Lands. Temp. Merneptaḥ. (L. D. Text, No. 38.) Parents, Khaʿemteri, Scribe of soldiers, and Tamy. Wives, Raʿya, Chief of the harim of Sobk, and Nebttaui.
Steward of the royal wife Nebtu. Temp. Tuthmosis III. Parents, Tetires and Ipu. Wife, Resti.
First prophet of Khons. Ramesside. Wife, Tausert, Chief of the harim of Khons.
Overseer of the treasury in the Ramesseum in the estate of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 29.) Wife, Meryēsi.
Chief steward of the divine adoratress ʿAnkhnesneferebreʿ. Temp. Apries and Amasis. (Inaccessible.) Parents, Ḥarsiēsi, Chamberlain of the divine adoratress, and Tahibet (from cone).
Officer of the estate of Amūn. Ramesside.
Governor of the town, Vizier. Temp. Amenophis II. (HAY, No. 15.) Parents, [ʿAḥmosi] Ḥumay (tomb 224) and Nub. Wife, Wertmaʿetef.
Scribe of the treasury of the estate of Amūn. Ramesside. Wife, Ḥenutenkhunet.

First prophet of Menkheperreʿ (Tuthmosis III). Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 51.) Parents, Neferḥotep, First prophet of Amenophis II, and Tausert, Songstress of Monthu. Wives, Ruia and Mutia or May.
Chief steward of Amūn, Overseer of the granaries of Upper and Lower Egypt. Temp. Ramesses II. Wife, Esi.
Prophet, Chief lector. Saite. (L. D. Text, No. 20.) Mother, Namenkhesi, Sistrum-player of Amūn. Wife, Tedi.

Fourth prophet of Amūn in Thebes. Temp. Taharqa and Psammetikhos I. Parents, Espṭaḥ, Prophet of Amūn, Mayor of the City, and Esenkhebi. Wives, Uzarenes, Sistrum-player of Amen-Reʿ (name in tomb), Eskhons and Shepetenmut (names from cones).

First prophet of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 45, L. D. Text, Nos. 10, 11.) Parents, Roma, First and second prophet of Amūn, and Roma, Singer of Amūn. Wife, Mertesger, Chief of the harim of Amūn.

Chief steward of the divine adoratress. Temp. Psammetikhos I. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 56, L. D. Text, No. 25.) Parents, ʿAnkh-ḥor, Divine father, and De-ubasteiri, variant Teiri. Wife, Shepenernute (name in tomb 196).

Chief steward of the god's wife Amenardais I. Saite. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 54, L. D. Text, No. 23.) Parents, Pedemut, Scribe, and Estawert (names from statues, in Berlin Mus. 8163, see infra, p. 69, and Cairo Mus. Ent. 36711).
Scribe, Counter of the grain in the granary of divine offerings of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. Wife, Wazronpet.
Second prophet of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (L. D. Text, No. 18.) Parents, Puia and Neferi. Wives, Tanefert and Sensonb.
Viceroy of Kush, Governor of the South Lands. Temp. Amenophis IV to Tutʿankhamūn. (CHAMPOLLION, A, L. D. Text, No. 110.) Mother, Wenḥo.
Chief steward of Amūn in the Southern City. Temp. Ramesses I to Sethos I(?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 35.) Parents, Nefertiu, Judge, and Iny, Songstress of the Theban Triad. Wife, Nezem(t).
Captain of troops, Eyes of the King in the Two Lands of the Retenu. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II. Wife, Ḥenuttaui.
Overseer of the kitchen of the Lord of the Two Lands. Temp. Amenophis II(?).
wab-priest-in-front of Amūn. Ramesside. Father, Ḥori, wab-priest of Amūn. Wife, Isumut.

Steward of the First prophet of Amūn, Mery (tomb 95), temp. Amenophis II. Usurped by Ḏḥutemḥab, Head of the makers of fine linen(?) of the estate of Amūn, temp. Ramesses II (?). Mother (of Ḏḥout), Ḏḥout. Parents (of Ḏḥutemḥab), Unnūfer, Head of weavers of the Temple of Amūn, and Ḥsi. Wife (of Ḏḥutemḥab), Bek-khons, Songstress of the Theban Triad.
Steward, Overseer of the granaries of Upper and Lower Egypt. Temp. Amenophis III (?). Wife, Nefertkhaʿ.
Overseer of the royal harim. Temp. Amenophis III. (Inaccessible.) Parents, Neḥ, Judge, and Senenu. Wife, Maiay.
Chief steward, At the head of the King, Overseer of the cattle of Amūn. Temp. Amenophis III. Parents, Ith-taui, Overseer of the cattle of Amūn, and Mut-tuy, Royal concubine.
Chief scribe of Amūn. Probably temp. Ay. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 53, HAY, No. 11.) Parents, Neby, Servant of Amūn, and Iuy. Wife, Merytreʿ.
Divine father of Amen-Reʿ. Temp. Ḥaremḥab. Parents, Amenemonet, Divine father of Amūn, and Takhaʿt, Chief of the harim of Amūn. Wife, Rennutet.
First prophet of the royal ka of Tuthmosis I. Temp. Sethos I. Parents, Khensemḥab and Tausert, Songstress of Monthu. Wife, Ḥatshepsut called Shepset.
Scribe, Astronomer of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis IV(?). Wife, Tawi.
Agent of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 16 bis, L. D. Text, No. 78.) Parents, Yotefnūfer, Agent of Amūn, and Tetiemnūter. Wife, Sebknakht.

Sculptor of Amūn, temp. Tuthmosis IV to Amenophis III(?). Usurped by Kenro, wab-priest, Head of the magazine of Khons, early Dyn. XIX. Wife (of Ḥuy), Taenheruensi, (of Kenro), Tarenenu, Chief of the harim of Amūn.
Governor of the town and Vizier. Temp. Amenophis IV. Parents, Neby, Overseer of the cattle of Amūn in the Northern District, and Ipuia. Wife, Mery(t)ptaḥ.
Royal scribe, Child of the nursery. Temp. Amenophis II. (L. D. Text, No. 81.) Wife, Mutnefert, Royal concubine.
Royal scribe, Overseer of the granaries of Upper and Lower Egypt. Temp. Amenophis III. (L. D. Text, No. 80.) Wife, Tiyi.

Name unknown, temp. Amenophis III. Usurped by Amenḥotp, Overseer of the prophets of Amūn, and his son Amenemōnet, Temple-scribe of the Temple of Ramesses 'Beloved like Amūn', Dyn. XX. (L. D. Text, No. 43.) Wife (of Amenemōnet), Ḥenutʿanensu.
First prophet of Mut, Mistress of Asher. Early Dyn. XVIII. Parents, ...n, Overseer of the granary, and Tuiu, Royal concubine. Wife, Meryt.
Governor of the town and Vizier, and mother, Sent, Prophetess of Ḥatḥor. Temp. Sesostris I. (L. D. Text, No. 42.) Wife (of Antefoḳer), Sitsisobk. Mother (of Sent), Dui.
Governor of the town and Vizier. Temp. Tuthmosis III. Parents, ʿAmethu (tomb 83) and Taʿamethu. Wife, Thuiu. (L. D. Text, No. 44.)
Overseer of the Cabinet. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?).

Mayor of the Southern Lake and the Lake of Sobk. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. Father, Min, Overseer of the seal. Wife, Meryt, Nurse of the King's daughter Tiʿa, Chief of the harim of Sobk of Shedty. (L. D. Text, No. 48.)
Nurse of the King's son Amenḥotp. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. Father, Ḥeḳreshu, Tutor of the King's son. (L. D. Text, No. 47.)

Scribe of the royal accounts (?) in the Presence, Overseer of the granary, temp. Ḥatshepsut (?). Usurped by Imiseba, Head of the altar, Head of the temple-scribes of the estate of Amūn, temp. Ramesses IX. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 60, L. D. Text, No. 40, WILKINSON, No. 1, 'Aichesi' of Prisse.) Parents, Amenḥotp, Head of scribes of the Temple of Amen-reʿ in Karnak, and Mutemmeres. Wife, Te(n)tpapersetha.
Vizier. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. Wife, Rennai.
First prophet of Amūn. Temp. Ḥatshepsut. Parents, Ḥepu, Third lector of Amūn, and ʿAḥḥotp, Royal concubine. Wife, Amenḥotp (name from Silsila, L. D. iii. 28 [4 b]). (L. D. Text, No. 50.)

wʿab-priest of Amūn of Karnak, and of Mut of Asher, Dyn. XX. Usurped by Espaneferḥor, Head of the temple-scribes of the estate of Amūn, temp. Siamūn. Father (of Espaneferḥor), Iufenamūn. Wife (of Espaneferḥor), Tabekenmut, Singer of the ... of Mut.
Scribe of the fields of the Lord of the Two Lands of Upper and Lower Egypt. Temp. Tuthmosis IV (?). Wife, Ḥenuttaui.
Usurped by Amenmosi, Overseer of sandal-makers (?) of the estate of Amūn. Dyn. XXI. Father, Suʿawiamūn, same title. Wife, Kefenu.
Chief steward, Steward of Amūn. Temp. Ḥatshepsut. (L. D. Text, No. 46.) Parents, Raʿmosi and Ḥatnefer(t).
First prophet of Amūn in the Mortuary Temple of Tuthmosis III. Temp. Amenophis II. (L. D. Text, No. 45.) Parents, ʿAḥmosi, [First] prophet of Amūn, and Reʿy, Royal concubine.
Amenḥotp (?), Overseer of works on the two great obelisks in the Temple of Amūn, Chief steward, Veteran of the King. Temp. Ḥatshepsut.
Royal scribe, Commander of soldiers. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 3, L. D. Text, No. 53.) Wife, Mutiriy, Songstress of Thoth and Neḥemʿawat.
Second prophet of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 2, L. D. Text, No. 54, HAY, No. 4.) Mother, Paʿa. Wife, Roy.
Fan-bearer on the right of the King. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 1, WILKINSON, No. 17, L. D. Text, No. 55.) Wife, Nebttaui.

Child of the nursery, Overseer of works in the Temple of Amūn, Standard-bearer of the Lord of the Two Lands. Usurped by Roy, Overseer of sculptors of the Lord of the Two Lands. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 8 bis, L. D. Text, No. 62.) Wife (of Ptaḥemḥet), Meryt. Wife (of Roy), Raʿḥuy.
Royal scribe, Scribe of recruits. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 4, L. D. Text, No. 57, WILKINSON, No. 16, HAY, No. 23.) Mother, Ēsi. Wife, Ithuy.

Overseer of the granary of the Lord of the Two Lands, wʿab-priest in the Mortuary Temple of Tuthmosis III. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II (?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 7, L. D. Text, No. 60.) Father, Minnakht (tomb 87).
Overseer of the treasury, Royal scribe. Temp. Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 6, L. D. Text, No. 59, HAY, No. 21.) Wife, Takhaʿt.

[1st ed. Anena], Overseer of the granary of Amūn. Temp. Amenophis I to Tuthmosis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 5, WILKINSON, No. 14, HAY, No. 8.) Parents, Ineni, Judge, and Sit-ḏhout. Wife, ʿAḥḥotp, called Thuiu.
Scribe, Counter of the grain of Amūn, Steward of the Vizier. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (L. D. Text, No. 56, HAY, No. 16.) Parents, Ḏhutmosi, Overseer of lands, and Antef. Wife, Beketamūn.
Governor of the town and Vizier. Early temp. Tuthmosis III. (L. D. Text, No. 41, WILKINSON, No. 64.) Wife, Taʿamethu (name in tomb 131).

First royal herald, Overseer of the gate, temp. Tuthmosis III. Partly usurped by Mery (tomb 95), temp. Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 11, L. D. Text, No. 71, WILKINSON, No. 31, HAY, No. 19.) Parents (of Amunezeḥ), Siḏhout, Judge, and Resi. Wife (of Amunezeḥ), Ḥenutnefert.
Lieutenant-commander of soldiers. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 12, HAY, No. 20.) Mother, Tetires. Wife, Baki, Chief royal nurse.
First prophet of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III. Parents, Amenemḥēt and Taōnet, King's nurse.
Overseer of the granaries of Upper and Lower Egypt, Overseer of horses of the Lord of the Two Lands, Royal scribe. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (HAY, No. 17.) Father, Sen-ḏhout.

Lieutenant of the King, Standard-bearer of the Lord of the Two Lands. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 8, L. D. Text, No. 61.) Wife, Neit, Chief royal nurse, Governess of the god.
Steward in the Southern City. Temp. Amenophis III.

Standard-bearer of (the sacred bark called) 'Beloved-of-Amūn', Captain of troops of the police on the west of Thebes. Temp. Tuthmosis IV to Amenophis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 9 bis, L. D. Text, No. 63, HAY, No. 22.) Wives, Sensenbut and Tiy.
Captain of the troops ... , Overseer of horses. Temp. Tuthmosis IV to Amenophis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 8 ter, L. D. Text, No. 65, HAY, No. 13.)
Royal butler clean of hands. Temp. Amenophis II. (L. D. Text, No. 67.) Parents, Iamnūfer, Mayor of Neferusi, and Meryt, Songstress of Thoth. Wife, Ḥat.
Chief steward of the King. Temp. Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 8 quater, L. D. Text, No. 68, WILKINSON, No. 33, HAY, No. 18.) Mother, Amenemōpet, Royal nurse. Wife, Tadedetes.
called ʿAmy, First royal herald, Fan-bearer on the right of the King. Temp. Amenophis II (?). Mother, Sent.

First prophet of Amūn. (See also usurpation in tomb 84.) Temp. Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 14, L. D. Text, No. 70, HAY, No. 14.) Parents, Nebpeḥtireʿ, First prophet of Min of Koptos, and Ḥunay(t), Chief nurse of the Lord of the Two Lands (name from tomb 84). Wife, Dey.

Mayor of the Southern City. Temp. Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 13, WILKINSON, No. 34, HAY, No. 12.) Parents, [ʿAḥmosi] Ḥumay (tomb 224) and Nub. Wives, Sentnay and Sentnefert, Royal nurses, and Meryt.
First prophet of Amūn. Temp. Amenophis II (?). Father, Ḏḥutiḥotp, wʿab-priest, Overseer of sandal-makers of the Temple of Amūn.
Third prophet of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II (?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 9, L. D. Text, No. 69.) (Inaccessible.) Mother, a Chief nurse of the Lord of the Two Lands. Wife, Ḥenuttaui.
Overseer of the seal, Overseer of the gold-land of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III. Mother, Sit-ḏḥout. Wife, Taimau.

Governor of the town and Vizier. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 15, L. D. Text, No. 58, WILKINSON, No. 35.) Parents, Neferweben, [Vizier], wʿab-priest of Amūn, and Bet. Wife, Meryt.
[Royal butler] clean of hands. Temp. Amenophis II.
Royal scribe, Child of the nursery. Temp. Amenophis III. (Inaccessible.)
Governor of the town and Vizier. End of Dyn. XI. (L. D. Text, No. 36.) Wife (or mother), Maʿetnemti.
(See tomb 80.) Temp. Amenophis II.
Prophet of the noble ram-sceptre of Amūn. Dyn. XIX. Wife, Amenemopet.

Governor of the town and Vizier. Temp. Sethos I to Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 32, L. D. Text, No. 39, HAY, No. 7.) Parents, Nebneteru called Theri, Chief prophet of Amūn, and Merytreʿ, Chief of the harim of Amūn. Wife, Tiy, Chief of the harim of Amūn.
Royal scribe, Steward of the estate of Amenophis III 'Reʿ is brilliant'. Temp. Amenophis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 33, L. D. Text, No. 37.) Parents, Neby, Judge, and Ḥepu.
First prophet of Onuris. Temp. Tuthmosis IV(?). Wife, Sensonb.
Mayor of Thinis, Overseer of prophets of Onuris. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 34.) Mother, Say.
Royal butler, Royal herald. Temp. Ḥatshepsut to Tuthmosis III. Parents, Pesediri (?) and Keku. Wife, Baktḥ.
Scribe of divine writings in the estate of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II. Parents, Simut, Head of outline-draughtsmen, and Wiay. Wife, Iuy, Songstress of Bubastis.
Temp. Tuthmosis III. Usurped by ʿAshefytemweset, Prophet of Amūn 'Great of Majesty', Ramesside. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 59.) Father (of ʿAshefytemweset), Pentawer. Wife of (ʿAshefytemweset), Mutemwia.
wʿab-priest over-the-secrets of the estate of Amūn, Prophet in the Temple of Tuthmosis IV. Temp. Ramesses VIII. (WILKINSON, No. 2, HAY, No. 10.) Father, Bekenamtūn, wʿab-priest of Amūn. Wife, Esi.
Head of goldworkers of the estate of Amūn. Dyn. XX. Father, a wʿab-priest of Anubis.
No texts. Dyn. XIX.
Hereditary prince. Temp. Tuthmosis IV to Amenophis III (?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 10, L. D. Text, No. 64.)
Tomb, Dyn. XI, used by Zemutefʿankh, Outline-draughtsman of the House of Gold, fashioning the gods of the estate of Amūn. Dyn. XXI-XXII.
Fan-bearer on the right of the King. Temp. Amenophis III (?).
Name lost. Temp. Ḥatshepsut to Tuthmosis III.
Second prophet of Amūn. Temp. Amenophis III. Parents, Yuia and Thuiu (tomb 46 in the Valley of the Kings). Called Maḥu in GARDINER and WEIGALL, Cat.
First lector of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?).

with Chapels of Amenemḥet, both Overseers of the magazine of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III. Parents (of [Amen]ḥotp), ʿAmethu (tomb 83) and Taʿamethu. Father (of Amenemḥet), Neferḥotep, Prophet. Wife (of Amenemḥet), Esnub.
Scribe, Overseer of the granary, Counter of bread. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (L. D. Text, No. 84.) Wife, Ḥenu(t)iri.
Overseer of the magazine of the Lord of the Two Lands, Steward of the good god Tuthmosis I. Temp. Tuthmosis I. Wife, Taerdais.
First herald, Overseer of the estate of Amūn. Temp. Ḥatshepsut. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 22, L. D. Text, No. 77.) Mother, Tarunet.
Great commander of soldiers of the estate of Amūn. Saite (?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 23.)
Royal scribe, Overseer of all that grows. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?), usurped in Ramesside times. (L. D. Text, No. 76.) Parents, Wazmosi and ʿAḥmosi. Wives, Sensonb and Tetisonb.
Mayor of Edfu, Mayor of the City. Saite. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 24.) Father, Pedeamun.
Name lost. Temp. Tuthmosis III or IV.
Harbour-master in the Southern City. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?). Wife, Tuy.
(See tomb 61.) Temp. Tuthmosis III. (L. D. Text, No. 87.)
Great scribe of the King, Overseer of the treasuries of Taharqa. Temp. Taharqa. (L. D. Text, No. 83.) Mother, Thesmeḥitpert.
Chief of the weavers in the Ramesseum in the estate of Amūn on the west of Thebes. Temp. Ramesses II. Wife, Ḥunuro.
(1st ed. 135) Prophet of Amenaphis who navigates on the Sea of Amūn. Dyn. XIX. (L. D. Text, No. 79.) Father, Besuemopet, same title as deceased. Wife, Tabesi.
wʿab-priest in front of Amūn. Dyn. XIX.
Royal scribe ... of the Lord of the Two Lands. Dyn. XIX.
Head of works of the Lord of the Two Lands in every monument of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 91.) Parents, Bak, Head of works in the Place of Eternity, and Tekhu. Wife, Taikharu.
Overseer of the garden in the Ramesseum in the estate of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 29.) Wife, Neshaʿ.
wʿab-priest in front, First royal son in front of Amūn, Overseer of peasants of Amūn. Temp. Amenophis III. Father, Sheroy, Prophet of Ptaḥ and Ḥatḥor. Wife, Ḥenutnefert.
probably called Ḥefia, Goldworker, Portrait sculptor. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II. Wife, Tauy.
wʿab-priest of Amūn. Ramesside. Wife, Takhaʿ(t).
Overseer of works of Amen-reʿ in Karnak. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II (?). Parents, Menta, Overseer of the granary of Amon, and Ḏḥutnofer. Wife, Sitamon.
Name lost. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II (?). Wife, Tentkhesbed.
Head of the field-labourers. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?). Wife, Henuttaui.
Head of bowmen. Dyn. XVIII. Wife, ʿAḥḥotp.
Overseer of the granary of Amūn, Scribe, Counter of grain, tny of the god's wife (titles from cones). Temp. Tuthmosis III (?). (Inaccessible.) Wife, Suitnub (from cone).
Head of the masters of ceremonies(?) of Amūn, &c. Temp. Tuthmosis IV(?). Wife, Nefert.
Prophet of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses III to V. Parents, Thonnfer (tomb 158) and Nefertere. Wife, Tamert, Chief of the harim [of Amūn].
Royal scribe of the table of the Lord of the Two Lands, Overseer of the huntsmen of Amūn. Ramesside. Wife, Sitmut.
Overseer of cattle of Amūn. Late Dyn. XVIII. (Unfinished.) Wife, Iaet-ib, Royal concubine.
Scribe, Counter of cattle of the god's wife of Amūn, Steward of the god's wife. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. (Unfinished.) Wife, Nefertere. Parents, Nebnufer.
Name lost, late Dyn. XVIII. Usurped in Ramesside times(?).
Name lost. Temp. Sethos I (?).
Butler. Temp. Tuthmosis III(?).
Great herald of the King. Temp. Ḥatshepsut and Tuthmosis III. (HAY, No. 1.)
Captain of troops, Governor of the South Lands. Dyn. XIX. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 43.) Wife, Mia.
First prophet of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 42, L. D. Text, No. 7.) Wife, Takhaʿt, Chief of the harim of Amūn, Songstress of Isis.
Third prophet of Amūn. Probably temp. Ramesses III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 44, L. D. Text, No. 9.) Wife, Nefertere, Chief of the harim of Amūn.
Fourth prophet of Amūn. Dyn. XIX. Wife, Mutemwia.
True royal acquaintance. Saite. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 46.) Parents, Pedemut and Tahibet.
Bearer of the floral offerings of Amūn. Temp. Amenophis III(?). Parents, Guraru, Gardener of the divine offerings of Amūn, and Kay. Wife, Taḳemt. (L. D. Text, No. 13, HAY, No. 9.)
Mayor in the Southern City, Overseer of the granary of Amūn. Dyn. XVIII. (Inaccessible.) Wife, Mut-tuy (from cone).
Mayor of the Southern City, Royal scribe. Dyn. XIX. (Inaccessible.) Father(?), Ḥuy, Judge, Mayor. Wife, Nezemt-net.
Scribe of recruits. Temp. Tuthmosis III.
Goldworker and portrait-sculptor. Temp. Tuthmosis IV(?). Wife, Tentamentet, called Kay.
Overseer of works in Karnak, Overseer of cattle. Dyn. XX. Parents, Ipy, Chief prophet of Thoth, and Ḥati, Chief of the harim of Thoth. Wife, Tay, Chief of the harim of Thoth.
Name lost. Dyn. XVIII. (Unfinished.)
Divine father clean of hands, Chosen lector of the lord of the gods. Dyn. XIX. Wife, Merynub.
Head of the goldworkers of Amūn. Temp. Amenophis II. Parents, Sensonb and Tanub. Wife, Maʿetka, Divine adoratress of Amūn.
Scribe of recruits of the Ramesseum in the estate of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II.
Name lost. Dyn. XVIII. Wife, Esi.
Royal butler, Child of the nursery. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II (?). Mother, Ḥepu.
Scribe of the divine offerings of the Gods of Thebes. Dyn. XIX. Wife, Biathefu.
Priest in front of Mut. Dyn. XIX. Wife, Tazabu.
No name. Temp. Tuthmosis IV (?).
Servant clean of hands. Temp. Amenophis II to Tuthmosis IV.
Scribe of truth in the Ramesseum in the estate of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II (?). (Unfinished.) Father, Nebḥed, Scribe of the divine seal of the estate of Amūn.
Scribe of the treasury in the estate of Amen-reʿ. Temp. Ramesses II. Wife, Mutemwia.
Scribe, Counter of grain in the granary of divine offerings of Amūn. Temp. Ḥatshepsut. Parents, Yotef, Steward of the Lord of the Two Lands, and ʿAḥmosi. Wife, Sentnefert.
No name. Dyn. XIX. (Unfinished.) Accessible from tomb 179.

Head sculptor of the Lord of the Two Lands, and Ipuky, Sculptor of the Lord of the Two Lands. Temp. Amenophis III to IV. Parents (of Nebamūn), Neferḥet and Thepu; (of Ipuky), Senennūter and Netermosi. Wife of Ipuky (and probably of Nebamūn), Ḥenutnefert.
Scribe of the mat. Temp. Tuthmosis III. Wife, Sit-ḏḥout.

Chief steward, Steward in the house of Ramesses II. Temp. Ramesses II. Parents, Paser, Mayor of the Southern City, and Tuia. Wife, Bekmut. (Brother, Hunufer, Mayor of the Southern City, Steward in the temple of Amenophis I, with wife Inihy, may be owner of tomb 385.)
Mayor in the Southern City, Royal scribe. Temp. Ramesses II.
Hereditary prince, Divine chancellor. First Intermediate Period.
Nomarch. First Intermediate Period. Wife, Imy, Prophetess of Ḥatḥor.
wʿab-priest of Amūn. Dyn. XIX. Parents, ʿAshakhet (tomb 174) and Tazabu. Wife, Mutemonet.
Royal butler clean of hands, Steward. Temp. Amenophis IV.
Overseer of carpenters of the northern lake of Amūn, Head of goldworkers in the estate of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II. Wives, Netemḥab and Tentpa...
Divine father, Prophet of the head of the King. Saite (usurped from a Ramesside tomb). Parents, Pakharkhons, Divine father, and Meramūniotes, Sistrum-player of Amen-rēʿ. Wife, Tanub.
Chamberlain of the divine adoratress, Director of the festival. Temp. Psammetikhos I. Parents, Pedeḥor, Head of outline-draughtsmen, and Thesmutpert.
Steward of the Great Royal Wife Teye, called Senaʿa. Temp. Amenophis III to IV. Parents, Silḥed and Ruiu.
Magnate of the seal in the treasury of the estate of Amūn. Dyn. XIX. Wife, Tadetawert. Stela only, but numbered as a tomb.
Overseer of marshland-dwellers of the estate of Amūn, Scribe of the temple of Amūn. Dyn. XIX. Father, a wʿab-priest in front of Amūn, Scribe of divine offerings of Amūn. Wife, Nezemtmut.
Scribe of the treasury of the estate of Amūn. Dyn. XIX. Wife, Wertnefert.
Chief steward of Amūn. Saite. Parents, Ibi (tomb 36) and Shepenernōte.
Chief steward of the god's wife, the divine adoratress ʿAnkhnesneferebreʿ. Temp. Psammetikhos II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 55.) Parents, Psammethek and Tadedubaste.
Head of the magazine of Amūn in Karnak. Ramesside.
Overseer of the magazine. Dyn. XVIII. (Inaccessible.)
Governor of the deserts on the west of Thebes, Head of the regiment of Pharaoh. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 36, HAY, No. 5.) Wife, Tuy.
First royal herald. Temp. Tuthmosis IV to Amenophis III.
Prophet of Ptaḥ Lord of Thebes, Priest in front of Amūn. Dyn. XIX(?).
Divine father of Mut. Dyn. XIX.
Sailor of the first prophet of Amūn (title from cone). Dyn. XVIII.
Royal butler. Temp. Tuthmosis III(?) to Amenophis II(?).
Scribe of the Place of Truth. Ramesside.
Scribe of divine offerings of Amūn. Ramesside. Parents, Ḥemawen and Nebuy.
Divine father of Amen-reʿ. Ramesside.
(?) Hereditary prince, Sole beloved friend. Saite. (formerly read Ḥatashemro)
Servant in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XIX. (L. D. Text, No. 104.) Parents(?), Piay, Sculptor in the Place of Truth, and Nefertkhaʿ. Wife, Nebtyunu.
Servant of the Lord of the Two Lands in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XIX. Parents, Nefersenut, same title as deceased, and Iuy. Wife, Waʿbe(t).
(See tomb 7.) Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 98.)
Servant of the Lord of the Two Lands, Servant in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XX. Parents, Bald (tomb 298) and Taysen. Wife, Nebtnuhet.
Custodian in the Place of Truth, Servant of Amūn in Luxor. Ramesside. Wife, Tawert.
Royal scribe in the Place of Truth. (Burial Chamber is tomb 265.) Dyn. XIX. Parents, Minmosi and Esi (names in tomb 335). Wife, Ḥatḥor, called Ḥunuro. (L. D. Text, No. 100.)
Foreman. Temp. Ramesses II to Sethos II. Parents, Nebnufer (tomb 6) and Iy. Wife, Webekht. (L. D. Text, No. 100.)
Sculptor. Temp. Ramesses II. Parents, Piay and Nefertkhaʿ (names in tomb 210). Wife, Duammeres.
Servant in the Place of Truth on the west of Thebes. Ramesside. Parents, Nebenmaʿet, Hr-mnw of Amūn, and Ḥetepti. Wife, Iymway.
Servant in the Place of Truth on the west of Thebes. Ramesside. Parents, Amennakht (tomb 218) and Iymway (name in tomb 218). Wife, Mertesger.
Servant in the Place of Truth. Ramesside. Parents, Amennakht (tomb 218) and Iymway. Wife, Nefert(em)satet (name from statue in Leyden Mus. D. 18).
Scribe of soldiers in the palace of the King on the west of Thebes. Ramesside.
First prophet of Monthu Lord of Thebes. Temp. Ramesses III to IV. Wife, Wiay, Chief of the harim of Monthu.
First ḳk-priest. Saite. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 17, L. D. Text, No. 93.)

Overseer of the estate of the god's wife, Overseer of the two granaries of the god's wife ʿAḥmosi Nefertere. Temp. Tuthmosis III or Hatshepsut. Parents, Senusert and Taidy. Wife, Nub, Royal concubine (in tombs 29 and 96).
First prophet of Hathor. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?). Wife, Webekht.
Royal scribe, Overseer of the royal nurses. Temp. Amenophis III.
Name lost. Temp. Tuthmosis III. Father, Hepu, Third lector of Amun.
Scribe of the treasury of Amun. Dyn. XVIII. Father (probably), Camethu (tomb 83).
Name lost. Dyn. XVIII. (Unfinished.)
Perhaps Menes, Scribe of soldiers of the Lord of the Two Lands (from cones). Dyn. XVIII. (Unfinished.)
Scribe, Counter of the grain of Amun in the granary of divine offerings. Early Dyn. XVIII. Wife, Nefertere.
Scribe of the divine seal of the treasury of Amun. Ramesside. Father, Weshebamunḥeref.
Royal scribe of the table of the Lord of the Two Lands. Ramesside.
Mayor. Dyn. XVIII or XIX. Wife, Ani.
First prophet of Monthu Lord of Thebes. Dyn. XX.
Second prophet of Amun, Overseer of the treasury of Amun. Ramesside.
Chief lector. Ramesside.
Royal butler clean of hands. Dyn. XVIII.
Governor of all Northern Lands. Temp. Tuthmosis IV to Amenophis II (?). Wife, Ḥetepti.
Overseer of sealers. Temp. Mentuḥotp-Nebḥepetreʿ. (L. D. Text, No. 14, New York, M.M.A. Excav. No. 517.) Parents, Iku and Nebti.
Scribe of the divine writings, Child of the nursery, Head of mysteries in the House of the morning. Temp. Tuthmosis III(?). Wife, ʿAlimosi.
Chamberlain of the divine adoratress ʿAnkhnesneferebreʿ. Saite. Wife, Tadepanehep. Father, Pedeamonnai; mother, Mutardais. (L. D. Text, No. 22.)
Mayor of the Southern City, called Ragi, Royal scribe. Saite. Father, a prophet and Izeneku-priest in Southern On.
Overseer of carpenters of the Temple of Amun. Ramesside.
Scribe, Steward of the royal wife. Dyn. XVIII. (Blocked.)
Scribe. Dyn. XVIII. Wife, Sitmenḥit.
Scribe, Counter of the cattle of Amun. Dyn. XVIII. Wife, Sitamon.
Maker of offerings of Tuthmosis III. Dyn. XVIII. Wife, Tamert.
Purveyor(?) of date-wine. Temp. Tuthmosis IV(?).
(See tomb 7.) Temp. Ramesses II.
Royal scribe, Overseer of the cattle of Amun, Overseer of the magazine of Amun. Temp. early Tuthmosis III. Father, Nesu-t, Head of the magazine of Amun.
Steward, Nurse of the god's wife. Temp. Hatshepsut. Parents, see tomb 71 (brother Senenmut). Wife, Senemiʿoḥ (name in tomb 71).
Scribe, Counter of the grain (a) in the granary of Amun, (b) of the granary of divine offerings. Temp. Amenophis III (?). Wife, Tannfer.
Scribe of the treasury, Custodian of the estate of Teye in the estate of Amun. Late Dyn. XVIII. Wife, Tamert.
Royal scribe, Steward in the estates of Haremhab, and of Amun. Temp. Haremhab (?). Wife, Nebttaui, nickname Towey. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 52, HAY, No. 2.)
Overseer of the cabinet, Fanbearer, Child of the nursery. Temp. Amenophis II. Wife, Ryu. (L. D. Text, No. 31.)

Scribe, Counter of the grain of Amun, temp. Tuthmosis IV to Amenophis III. Usurped by Maḥu, Deputy in the mansion of Usimare-setepenre (= Ramesseum) in the estate of Amun, temp. Ramesses II. Father (of Maḥu), perhaps Piay. Wife (of Maḥu), Tawert. (L. D. Text, No. 32.)
Child of the nursery, Royal scribe of the house of the royal children. Temp. Tuthmosis IV (?). Mother, Nay.

Warb-priest, Scribe in all the monuments of the estate of Amun, Head of the outline-draughtsmen in the House of Gold of the estate of Amun. Ramesside. Parents, Ḥuy, warb-priest of Amun, and Beketptaḥ. Wife, Mutemwia.
Scribe, Weigher of [Amun], Overseer of the ploughed lands of [Amun]. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?). Wife, Nubemweset (name from cone).
Warb-priest of Amenophis I (name and title from cones, as no texts in tomb). Dyn. XVIII.
An Overseer of the fields. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?).
Scribe of the granary in the Temple of Amun, Scribe of accounts in the Ramesseum. Temp. Ramesses II. Wife, Webekht.
Overseer of cattle, Chief of the Lord of the Two Lands. Dyn. XIX.
(See tomb 215, which is the Chapel.) Dyn. XIX.
Chief craftsman of the Lord of the Two Lands in the Place of Truth on the west of Thebes. Dyn. XIX. Parents, Buḳentef and Iy (names in tomb 219). Wife, Ḥenutrayunu.

Officer of the workmen in the Place of Truth on the west of Thebes, Fashioner of the images of all the gods in the House of Gold. Dyn. XX. (L. D. Text, Nos. 102-3.) Parents, Amennakht and Tārekhʿan. Wife, Ḥenutmet.
Family tomb of Nebnakht. Servant in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XIX. Parents, Ipy and ʿAuti (names from stela in Turin Mus. Sup. 6044). Wife, Thay.
Name lost. Ramesside.
Warb-priest, Lector of Ptaḥ-Sokari. Dyn. XIX. Destroyed.
Royal scribe. Temp. Ay.
Divine father of Amūn in the West, Lector in the Temple of Sokari. Ramesside.
Scribe in the estate of his Lord. Ramesside.
First prophet of Monthu of Tod, and of Thebes, sem-priest in the Ramesseum in the estate of Amūn. Ramesside. (Inaccessible.) Wife, ...y.
Head warb-priest, Divine father in the Temples of Amenophis III, and of Sokari. Ramesside.
Overseer of the treasury of gold and silver, Judge, Overseer of the cabinet. Temp. Tuthmosis IV (?). Parents, Nekhu (?) and ʿAḥḥotp. Wife, Ḥenutyunu.
Divine father of the mansion of Amenophis III. Ramesside. Mother, Tazesertka. Wife, Nefertere.
Herdsman of Amen-Reʿ. Ramesside. Wife, Tay, Songstress of Mut.
Chief steward of the god's wife. Temp. Psammetikhos I. Parents, Pedubaste, Divine father beloved of the god, and Tasentenḥor.

location_sub_area: In valley south of Deir el-Bahari Temples
notes_from_pm: Chief steward in ..., Chancellor. Temp. Mentuḥotp (Sʿankhkareʿ). (Formerly read Meḥenkwetreʿ.) Son Antef, Hereditary Prince.
Unfinished Temple of Mentuḥotp-Sʿankhkareʿ. See Bibl. ii, p. 135.
Head of bowmen, Overseer of the South Lands. Ramesside.
First prophet of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II to Sethos II. Wife, Tamut. (name in niche in Court).
Scribe of the offerings of all the gods. Ramesside. (Reused.) Father, Raʿy. Wife, Bek(et)werner.
Head of the magazine of Mut. Ramesside. Wife, Tentonet, Songstress of Mut.
Scribe of the table. Ramesside. Parents, Roro and Esi. Wife, Tabes.
Wab-priest of Amūn. Ramesside.
Scribe of the divine book of Khons. Ramesside. Re-used by Setau (tomb 289).
Viceroy of Kush, Overseer of the South Lands. Temp. Ramesses II. Father, Siwazyt. Wife, Nefertmut, Chief of the harin of Nekhbet.
Servant in the Place of Truth on the West. Ramesside. Parents, Siwazyt, Head of the bark of Amūn, and Tausert Meḥytkhacti. Wife, [name unclear in source].

Servant in the Great Place, and Nekhtmin, Servant in the Place of Truth. Late Dyn. XVIII. Parents (of Nu), Pia and Mutnefert, and wife, Khatnesut. Parents (of Nekhtmin), Min-hotep and Nefertere, and wife, Sekhmet.
Servant in the Place of Truth. Temp. Sethos I to Ramesses II. Father, Ḥenekhu (from stela in Brit. Mus. 262). Wife, Makhay.
First prophet of Amun. Temp. Ramesses IV. Father, Merubaste, Chief steward of the Lord of the Two Lands (name and title on statue in Cairo Mus. 42162).
Overseer of the granary of Amun, temp. Amenophis III. Usurped by Roma, wab-priest of Amun, early Ramesside. Wife (of Roma), Hathor.

Head of the secrets in the Chest of Anubis, sem-priest in the Good House, Embalmer. Temp. Tuthmosis IV to Amenophis III (?). Parents, Sennuter, sem-priest in the Good House, &c., and Senemiʿoḥ. Wives, Nefertere and Rennutet.
Scribe of the divine offerings of all the gods, Officer of the treasury ... in the Southern City. Ramesside. Wives, Maʿetmut, Sekhemui, and Nefertere.
Scribe, Counter of grain of Amun, Overseer of fields (name and titles from cones). Early Dyn. XVIII.

Foreman in the Place of Truth, and father (probably), Unnufer, Servant of the Lord of the Two Lands in the Place of Truth [&c.]. Ramesside. Wife (of Baki), Taysen (name in tomb 213). Wife (of Unnufer), Maia.
Foreman of the Lord of the Two Lands in the Place of Truth [&c.]. (Also owner of tomb 359.) Temp. Ramesses III and IV. Father, Hay, Foreman in the Place of Truth. Wife, Wab.
Viceroy of Kush, Governor of the South Lands, Scribe of the table of the Lord of the Two Lands. Ramesside. Wife, Hunuro.
Scribe of the table of the Lord of the Two Lands in the estate of Amun. Ramesside. Wife, (name in cartouche). (Name and titles copied by GREENLEES, in Philadelphia Univ. Mus.)
Overseer of the magazine. Ramesside. Father, Userḥat, Head of the magazine of Amun. (Description by GREENLEES, in Philadelphia Univ. Mus.)
Head of the magazine [of Amun?], Third prophet of Amun. Dyn. XIX-XXI.
Scribe of the table of Amun, Scribe of the Lord of the Two Lands. Ramesside. For position, see p. 356.
Wʿab-priest in front of Amun, Scribe of the divine offerings of Amun. Dyn. XIX-XXI. Wife, Tamelḥit.
Door-opener of the estate of Amun. Dyn. XIX-XXI. Wife, Mutenopet. (Copies of texts by GREENLEES, in Philadelphia Univ. Mus.)
(name from ushabti). Dyn. XX-XXI. (Unfinished.) (Description by GREENLEES, in Philadelphia Univ. Mus.)
Unique royal concubine, Prophetess of Ḥatḥor. Temp. Mentuḥotp (Nebḥepetreʿ). Deir el-Bahari, in the Temple of Mentuḥotp. (NAVILLE, No. 10.)
Name unknown. Dyn. XIX-XXI. (Blocked.)
A Chancellor of the King of Lower Egypt. Dyn. XI.
Seal-bearer of the King of Lower Egypt. Temp. Mentuḥotp-Nebḥepetreʿ.
Governor of the town and Vizier. Saite. In court of a Dyn. XI tomb. Mother, Irterau (tomb 390).
Great steward. Temp. Mentuḥotp-Nebḥepetreʿ and Mentuḥotp-Sʿankhkareʿ.
Seal-bearer of the King of Lower Egypt, Henchman. Dyn. XI. Mother, Sentshe.
Governor of the town and Vizier, Judge. Temp. Mentuḥotp-Nebḥepetreʿ.
Custodian of the bow. Dyn. XI. Mother, Nebtiotef. Wife(?), Mery(t).
Scribe of the counting of corn in the granary of divine offerings of Amun. Temp. Tuthmosis III(?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 25.) Parents, Senires, Mayor, and Taiy. Wife, Titau.
Necropolis-stonemason of Amun. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Hatshepsut(?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 26.) Wife, Henut.
Daughter of Mentuḥotp-Sʿankhibtaui and Iʿob, wife of Mentuḥotp-Nebḥepetreʿ.
Perhaps wife of Amosis. (Royal Cache, Dyn. XXI, see DEIR EL-BAHARI, Bibl. i 2, Pt. 2, in the Press.)
Servant in the Place of Truth. Ramesside. Parents (perhaps), Busentef and Iy (names in tomb 219 (5)). Wife, Maani.
Servant in the Place of Truth. Ramesside. Wife, Tentnubt.
Outline-draughtsman of Amun in the Place of Truth, and in the Temple of Sokari. Temp. Sethos I. Parents, Amenemḥet, Outline-draughtsman in the Temple of Sokari, and Mutnefert. Wife, Nefertere.
Overseer of the prophets of all the gods, Chief prophet of Sobk, Scribe of the Temple of Monthu. Ramesside. Mother, Nefertere. Wife, Iuy.
Possibly Smen (from cones). Dyn. XVIII.
Foreman. (See tomb 3.) Ramesside.
Servant in the Place of Truth. Ramesside. Wife, Tuy.
Servant in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XX. Wife, Tatemeḥet.

Mosi and Annexed tomb of Mosi probably his grandson, and Ipy, perhaps his son, all Servants in the Place of Truth. Ramesside. Wife (of Mosi, tomb 329), Ḥenutwact. Father (of Mosi, Annexed tomb), Icoḥnufer. Wife (of Mosi, Annexed tomb), Patet (name on stela, Louvre, C. 280, see infra). Wife (of Ipy), Bakt.
Servant in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XIX. Parents, Simut and Peshedu. Wife, Takhaʿ.
Chief prophet of Monthu. Ramesside. Father, Ḥatiay (tomb 324). Wife, Maiay. Chief of the harim of Monthu.
Chief watchman of the granary of the estate of Amun. Ramesside.
Name lost. Temp. Amenophis III (?).
A Chief of husbandmen. Temp. Amenophis III (?).
Warb-priest of Amenophis (I) Lord of the Two Lands, Chiseller of Amun, Servant in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XIX. (L. D. Text, No. 105.) Parents, Piay and Nefertkha. Wife, Nubemsheset.
Servant in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XIX. Parents, see tomb 335 (brother). Wife, Huynefert.
Chiseller in the Place of Truth (see tomb 4), temp. Ramesses II. Usurped by Eskhons. Dyn. XXI or XXII.
Outline-draughtsman of Amun. Late Dyn. XVIII. Wife, Tamyt.

Servant in the Place of Truth, and Peshedu, Servant in the Place of Truth, Necropolis-stonemason of Amun in Karnak. Temp. Ramesses II. Parents (of Huy), Seba and Nefer[t]iyti (names from stelae in Brit. Mus. 446 and Louvre, C. 86). Father (of Peshedu), Harmosi. Wife (of both), Takharu.
Servant in the Place of Truth (perhaps also owner of tomb 354). Early Dyn. XVIII. Parents, Macenhmut(?) and Hut. Wives, Reditico and Nubnefert.
Head of the altar in the Ramesseum. Temp. Ramesses II. Wife, Kemenʿa.
Hereditary prince, Royal herald. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 19, HAY, No. 6.) Mother, Tabenert. Wife, Tepiḥu.
called Paḥekmen, Overseer of works, Child of the nursery. Early Dyn. XVIII. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 37, L. D. Text, No. 74.) Parents, Irtonena and Tirukak.
Overseer of the herds of Amen-Reʿ in the Southern City; Royal scribe of the herds of Amenophis I. Ramesside. Wife, Tawert.
wʿb-priest, Eldest king's son of Tuthmosis I. Temp. Tuthmosis I. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 30, L. D. Text, No. 75.) Parents, Senidhout and Takhrod. Wife, Renay.

Overseer of the women of the royal harim of the divine adoratress Tentōpet, temp. Ramesses IV. Probably usurped from Penrēʿ, Chief of Mezay, Overseer of the Lands of Syria (name on cones in Pit in Court), [temp. Ramesses II].
Scribe of the nome. Ramesside. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 20.) Wife, Nefertere.

A Chief steward, Unique friend, Mayor, Dyn. XVIII. Usurped by Naʿamutnakht, Door-opener of the House of Gold of Amūn, Dyn. XXII. Wife (of original owner), Diʿankh. Parents (of Naʿamutnakht), Nezem, same title, and Suru (?).
Overseer of fowl-houses. Early Dyn. XVIII. Mother, Ipu. Wife, Amenhotp.
Scribe of the counting of bread. Dyn. XVIII. Wife, Nefertwaḥ, Nurse of the King's son Menkheperureʿ (Tuthmosis IV).
Scribe of horses. Ramesside. (Inaccessible.) Wife, Ay.
An Overseer of the granary of Amūn. Ramesside. (Inaccessible.)
(See tomb 71.) Temp. Ḥatshepsut.
No texts. Perhaps Amenemḥet (tomb 340, cf. box-lid in Finds of this tomb). Early Dyn. XVIII.
Perhaps Amenpaḥaʿpi, Servant in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XX. Mother, Ḥunuro. Wife, Ḥenutenkhune.
Servant in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XIX. Father, ʿAmak, Servant in the Place of Truth. Wife, Wazronpet.
Servant in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XIX. Wife, Wernuro.

location_sub_area: In Court of Temple of Ḥatshepsut
notes_from_pm: Daughter of Tuthmosis III, wife of Amenophis II. (New York, M.M.A. Pit 65.) In Court of Temple of Ḥatshepsut.
(See tomb 299 with footnote.) Temp. Ramesses III and IV. (L. D. Text, No. 108, WILKINSON, No. 10.)
Foreman in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. Parents, Ḥuy (tomb 361) and Taneḥesi. Wife, Tuy.
See also Tomb 20, supra, p. 546. Sarcophagus as Queen-Consort, quartzite, in Cairo Mus. Ent. 47032.
Probably Princess Neferureʿ, daughter of Ḥatshepsut.
Temp. Tuthmosis III.
Wife of King Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa. Found by Mariette in 1859.
Daughter of ʿAḥḥotp (wife of King Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa).
Tomb of Queen ʿAḥmosi Nefertere (probably). Attributed to Amenophis I by Carter, later equated by Černý with 'House of Amenophis of the Garden'.
Eldest son of King Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa and ʿAḥḥotp.
Found by Mariette in 1860. Pyramid, see Hay MSS. 29816.
Probably younger brother and successor of Antef (Sekhemreʿ-Wepmaʿet).
Royal acquaintance. Dyn. XVII. Objects found by Vassalli in 1863.
Found by Mariette in 1857. For pyramid, possibly of Kamosi, see infra, p. 620.
Wife of King Ḏḥuti. Found in tomb by Passalacqua.
Unfinished.
Scribe of the Great Harîm, probably temp. Antef (Nubkheperrēʿ). Rock-tomb, uninscribed. Found by Mariette in 1860, probably near Theb. tb. 13.
Pyramid behind Theb. tb. 24, perhaps belonging to this tomb.
